topic: Timestamp Error tab — April 2026 methodology.json update block (c2 after ISS-1085/1086/1090 patches)
change_summary: Decider applied three c1-derived patches to the ts-april-2026-update block in build-scripts/generate-html.js: (a) ISS-1085 tightened the partial-sample extrapolation so 'column would still be empty' is replaced by 'As of 2026-04-17...' with a falsifiable prediction about WIN-035–WIN-039; (b) ISS-1086 added a parenthetical reconciliation noting WIN-035–WIN-039 are not in the author's classified sample; (c) ISS-1090 softened the force-push framing to 'detectable through GitHub server logs and public forks, but without leaving a verifiable trace on the Bitcoin chain.' Block length 7,727 chars, 5 body paragraphs + TLDR.
our_argument_summary: The updated block credits methodology.json (commit 6e06efb) for narrowing the OpenTimestamps scope rhetoric and publishing a five-class claim taxonomy, while maintaining that the structural fix (predictions-only file with its own independent OTS anchor) is still not implemented. It turns the author's own taxonomy against the headline count using eight specific classifications from the partial claim_index.json sample, now correctly caveated that WIN-035–WIN-039 (the dome's prior self-declared prospective set) have not been classified in that sample. The block frames the remaining gap as rhetorical-correct-but-structural-unfixed and offers an explicit falsification condition (if the author ships a predictions-only OTS-anchored file that provably predates the observations file, this tab should be rewritten).

tldr_review.notes: TLDR accurately compresses the updated block: credits rhetorical scoping + taxonomy, identifies missing structural fix (predictions-only file with independent OTS anchor), names the headline '69 confirmed' as continuing to conflate classes the author has now distinguished. No factual error. One subtle rhetorical point: the TLDR uses 'rhetorical position is now correct' rather than 'documentary position' — this inherits the framing imprecision flagged in ISS-1088 (still open), but does not introduce a new error.

iss_1085_extrapolation_fix.notes: Recommended replacement text is present in full. The closing sentence of the taxonomy paragraph now reads: 'As of 2026-04-17, the prospective_confirmed column contains no confirmed WINs — only one pending prediction (pred-eclipse-tier3). The column could fill out as more of the 69 WINs get classified in claim_index.json; a test of the methodology’s good faith will be whether WIN-035–WIN-039 (the author’s own previously-declared prospective set) are tagged prospective_confirmed when classification is extended.'

iss_1086_win035_039_reconciliation.notes: Reconciliation added as a parenthetical inside the taxonomy paragraph rather than a standalone sentence. Current text: '(Note: the dome author’s own earlier self-declared prospective set — WIN-035 through WIN-039 — has not yet been classified in claim_index.json as of this writing; the partial sample is the author’s curated strongest-current-targets set, not his prospective subset.)' Substantive content matches recommendation, slightly reworded. Acceptable.

iss_1090_force_push_softening.notes: Current text: 'both of which are only git-versioned and can be force-pushed — detectable through GitHub server logs and public forks, but without leaving a verifiable trace on the Bitcoin chain.' Adds 'leaving a' vs recommended 'without a verifiable trace' — equivalent in substance, slightly more readable.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — prose block. The verifiable data claims are (a) commit hash 6e06efb for methodology.json, (b) four verbatim quotes from methodology.json, (c) eight WIN classifications from claim_index.json, (d) pred-eclipse-tier3 timing (August 2026).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The '69 confirmed' figure is the dome's public V51.1 headline, not our internal wins.json count (72 entries). The block correctly treats this as the dome author's self-presentation.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Counterpoint paragraph explicitly concedes we cannot prove our review caused the dome author's update. The closing paragraph offers an unwind condition (structural fix → rewrite with full credit). Both are appropriate epistemic hedges.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None material. Agent_rules paraphrasing is the only live-file discrepancy (see citation_check below).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section/tab rewrite, not a WIN with monitoring automation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N/A — this is our own prose.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: N/A — this is our own prose.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: N/A — this is our own prose.

holes_found.description: Registry_counts self-caveat still not engaged (c1 hole #3 → ISS-1087 still open). The live methodology.json includes explicit self-caveat language about registry_counts being 'bookkeeping for the current registry state' and 'not a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective audit' (verified live this review). The block's closing sentence — 'the site’s 94.5% headline and 69 confirmed tagline rest on a proof structure the author’s own documentation now concedes does not prove what the tagline implies' — is substantively correct but gives a defender an easy rhetorical reply: 'I said exactly that at the display layer in registry_counts. You are attacking a framing I have already disowned.' The remaining critique the block SHOULD be making is that the self-caveat lives only inside methodology.json and has not been propagated to the wins-page headline display that casual readers see. Leaving this unaddressed lets the defender paint us as reactive to an older state of the site.
holes_found.recommendation: Close ISS-1087 in the next decider pass. Add a clause to the taxonomy paragraph (or the closing paragraph): 'The author also labels the registry_counts display as bookkeeping rather than a prospective score — a display-level self-caveat that has not been propagated to the wins-page headline a casual reader sees.' This acknowledges the concession at the right level (inside methodology.json, not on the visible headline) without ceding the substantive critique.

holes_found.description: Rhetorical/structural framing still compresses documentary changes (c1 hole #4 → ISS-1088 still open). The block uses 'rhetorical' or 'rhetorical scope' in four places (summary TLDR, counterpoint reading, closing line). The author has, in fact, shipped substantive documentary/architectural changes: methodology.json itself, claim_index.json with machine-readable classifications, ai_manifest.json + llms.txt updates telling AI auditors how to score, agent_rules, registry_integrity_notes, review-response.html. These are not merely rhetorical — they are architectural changes to the site's epistemic surface. A defender can legitimately charge that calling them 'rhetorical' compresses real documentary work. The substantively correct split is documentary (what has been shipped) vs cryptographic (what is still missing: a predictions-only file with its own independent OTS anchor provably predating the observations file).
holes_found.recommendation: Close ISS-1088. Replace 'rhetorical' with 'documentary' in the three load-bearing locations (TLDR final clause, closing-paragraph summary, and the counterpoint paragraph). Reserve 'cryptographic' for the missing structural fix. This also naturally aligns with the author's own vocabulary (methodology.json, agent_rules, claim_index.json) and makes the remaining gap specific rather than dismissive.

holes_found.description: Agent_rules quotes are paraphrased, not verbatim (partial verification result for ISS-1089). WebFetch against the live methodology.json this review confirmed the three timestamp_scope / claim_level_rule quotes verbatim AND all eight WIN classifications verbatim. However, the four agent_rules are paraphrased: (1) Block: 'Do not assume OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective.' Live: 'Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective.' (drops 'that an'). (2) Block: 'Separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items before scoring.' Live: '...before scoring the model.' (drops 'the model'). (3) Block: 'Carry critical open problems forward instead of deleting them from evidence.' Live: 'Carry the critical open problems forward instead of deleting them from the strongest evidence case.' (drops 'the', 'strongest', 'case'). (4) Block: 'Use review-response.html when critique depends on archival prompt wording.' Live: 'Use review-response.html when a critique depends on archival prompt wording or older slogan-level page text.' (drops 'or older slogan-level page text'). A dome defender quoting the live file back can charge selective misquotation. Severity is minor because the substantive meaning is preserved in each case, but citations rendered in quotation marks should be verbatim.
holes_found.recommendation: Three options in order of cost: (a) lowest — add the word 'paraphrased' once where the agent_rules are introduced: 'The new agent_rules block goes further: paraphrased, the first rule reads...'; (b) middle — quote the agent_rules verbatim from the live file (restore dropped words); (c) highest — add a verification footnote to the block: 'Verbatim quotes and classifications verified against the live methodology.json and claim_index.json on 2026-04-17.' Option (b) is the cleanest. Option (c) would also close ISS-1089 substantively. ISS-1089 is currently assigned-analyst; the analyst should note that three timestamp_scope quotes + one claim_level_rule + eight WIN classifications are verbatim, but the four agent_rules are paraphrased and need correction.

holes_found.description: Missed opportunity to note that pred-eclipse-tier3 is itself disputed. The block says: 'The only item in the published sample tagged prospective_confirmed is pred-eclipse-tier3, which is a pending prediction for the August 2026 eclipse, not a confirmed WIN at all.' This is correct but leaves the dome author's prospective_confirmed tagging uncontested. Our own data/predictions.json has PRED-TIER-3 with is_genuinely_prospective: false, our_verdict: 'pending,' and crypto_hash: null — i.e., by our own analysis this prediction does not even meet the dome author’s own claim_level_rule for prospective_confirmed (a separately-timestamped numerical threshold that predates the confirming data pull). Noting this strengthens the argument: even the one prospective_confirmed label in the author's partial sample does not satisfy his own published rule.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional enhancement, not required. Could add one clause to the closing of the taxonomy paragraph: 'Our own cross-reference (data/predictions.json PRED-TIER-3) notes that even this item lacks a separate crypto_hash for the prediction parameters, so it does not yet satisfy the claim_level_rule the author just published.' Low priority — the block already does the primary work (it's a pending prediction, not a confirmed WIN); this would just sharpen the point.

holes_found.description: TLDR uses the word 'rhetorical' and closing uses the triad 'rhetorical scope is now correct.' If/when ISS-1088 is resolved, the TLDR and closing should be updated in the same pass to preserve internal consistency. Not a defect in the current c2 content given ISS-1088 is still open; flagged here so the fix is not forgotten.
holes_found.recommendation: Bundle the TLDR, counterpoint paragraph, and closing triad rewrite into the ISS-1088 fix so all 'rhetorical' instances transition to 'documentary' together.

stronger_arguments.description: The strongest version of the 'documentary vs cryptographic' framing leverages the author's own published claim_level_rule verbatim: 'For claim-level prospectivity, verify that the numerical threshold or directional wording predates the confirming data pull or public release.' Since that is the author's own test, the block can close on: 'The author has published both the rule and the infrastructure (git + OpenTimestamps + SHA-256). What is still missing is the application of the rule to his own registry — a predictions-only file whose OpenTimestamps receipt provably predates any status_history.json receipt that references the confirming data. The gap is not rhetorical and not technical; it is volitional.' That reframes the critique in the author's own vocabulary and makes it harder to deflect.
sources: existing 'What Would Fix It' section on the same tab

stronger_arguments.description: Internal consistency: the block's 'column still empty' claim could be cross-linked to predictions.json. Currently pred-eclipse-tier3 is the single item tagged prospective_confirmed. If our analyst integrates a verdict update when the August 2026 eclipse window closes, the block should anchor its falsification condition there: 'when the 2026-08-12 eclipse window closes, pred-eclipse-tier3 will receive a verdict in our predictions.json. That date is the first live test of whether the author’s prospective_confirmed column actually holds a genuine prospective-prediction WIN — separable from the five non-prospective classes he himself has labeled.' This turns the block into a live, dated prediction of our own.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider’s three patches were surgically correct and closed the two major holes the c1 review identified. The 'column still empty' rhetorical move — where our closing sentence extrapolated from a partial sample — has been rewritten as a dated, falsifiable statement about WIN-035–WIN-039 future classifications. The WIN-035–WIN-039 reconciliation removes the internal-consistency charge between the ts-scope block and the taxonomy paragraph. The force-push softening now matches the Section 1.4 body's framing. These are the three highest-leverage fixes c1 identified, and they were applied cleanly.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is our own prose, and 'doesn’t save the claim' is a WIN-review frame. The equivalent here is: the patches are correct but incomplete. Two c1 holes (ISS-1087 registry_counts self-caveat, ISS-1088 rhetorical-vs-documentary framing) remain open, and the agent_rules quotes were found to be paraphrased rather than verbatim. The block is materially stronger than c1 but has three residual issues that a determined dome defender can still exploit.

deeper_analysis:
The c2 review confirms the decider's three issue-derived patches (ISS-1085/1086/1090) were applied accurately and the c1's two major findings are closed. The block's structure is unchanged and remains well-calibrated: credit the concession, specify the structural gap, offer a falsification condition. Live-file verification via WebFetch to methodology.json and claim_index.json confirms (a) the three timestamp_scope / claim_level_rule quotes verbatim, (b) all eight WIN classifications verbatim, and (c) confirms WIN-035–WIN-039 are not present in claim_index.json as the new parenthetical now correctly states. The partial-sample extrapolation language is substantively improved and now reads as a falsifiable near-term prediction (will WIN-035–WIN-039 be tagged prospective_confirmed when classified?) rather than a rhetorical claim about the universe of WINs.

Three residual issues were identified in c2, none of which are show-stoppers:

1. ISS-1087 (registry_counts self-caveat) remains open. The live methodology.json contains explicit bookkeeping-vs-audit self-caveat language that the block does not engage with. Adding a ~15-word clause to the taxonomy or closing paragraph would close this cleanly.

2. ISS-1088 (rhetorical vs documentary framing) remains open. The block uses 'rhetorical' in four load-bearing locations (TLDR, counterpoint paragraph, closing triad, opening clause). Replacing with 'documentary' and reserving 'cryptographic' for the missing piece is straightforward search-and-replace plus a light rewrite of the closing triad.

3. Agent_rules are paraphrased, not verbatim. WebFetch comparison shows all four are lightly edited. Easiest fix: restore the live-file wording. Alternative: introduce the quotes as 'paraphrased' instead of as direct quotes. ISS-1089 is assigned to the analyst; this review adds the verification result and classification details.

A new finding: the block's one-sentence dismissal of pred-eclipse-tier3 ('a pending prediction for the August 2026 eclipse, not a confirmed WIN at all') is correct but leaves an opportunity on the table. Our own data/predictions.json already records PRED-TIER-3 with is_genuinely_prospective: false and crypto_hash: null, meaning that by the dome author's own published claim_level_rule (numerical threshold that predates the confirming data pull, with a separable timestamp) this prediction does not yet satisfy prospective_confirmed. Noting this turns the block's argument into a self-reinforcing loop where even the one prospective_confirmed tag in the author's sample fails the author's own test. Minor enhancement, not required.

Counterpoint reading (per user-preference instruction 'think about the other side of the argument too'): the strongest dome-defender counter to the updated block is the ISS-1088 compression. A defender can legitimately say: 'I have shipped methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules, registry_integrity_notes, and review-response.html. Those are documentary architecture changes, not just rhetoric. Your review is calling them rhetorical because conceding they are documentary would force you to admit I have shipped more than your tab acknowledges.' That charge lands only as long as the block's text uses 'rhetorical' rather than 'documentary.' Closing ISS-1088 by terminology replacement is therefore the single highest-value remaining edit.

The block's recommended_action is minor_edit: no major structural change needed, no verdict flip, no rewrite. Three small patches (one each for ISS-1087, ISS-1088, ISS-1089) will close the residual issues. The block is already publishable in its current form — the residual issues are refinements, not factual errors.

Substantive meaning preserved; strict verbatim quoting fails.
